concept: Cap the compound's activity with a metabolically labile self-inactivating group (a cleavable ester/thioester positioned so it is cleaved once the drug leaves the target site), so local activity is preserved while systemic exposure is rapidly detoxified.
mechanism: Blood and hepatic esterases / CYP3A rapidly cleave the labile group to an inactive carboxylic-acid metabolite (the 'soft drug' / antedrug principle), collapsing systemic activity while sparing the local effect.

citation.label: Remien K, Patel P, Bowman A. Fluticasone. StatPearls [Internet]. StatPearls Publishing; 2024.
citation.note: Fluticasone propionate is metabolized to its inactive 17-beta-carboxylic acid, limiting systemic glucocorticoid exposure.
